title: No RAGrets
subtitle: M.S. NLP Capstone — Industry-Sponsored Graduate Capstone — Featured by Santa Cruz Works
description:
Industry-sponsored graduate capstone completed in partnership with CarbonBridge. Built an applied NLP system for scientific literature triage, extraction, and knowledge graph exploration to support research in sustainable fuel innovation.
- Developed an open-source pipeline for extracting entities, relationships, and technical concepts from scientific literature
- Integrated Docling and Qwen vision-language models for document parsing and information extraction
- Built a React-based interface for graph exploration and AI-assisted literature review
- Set up FastAPI, Dgraph, and containerized infrastructure to support continued use and future development
- Documented architecture and technical decisions to enable project handoff to future contributors

title: EduMUSE
description:
Built an AI-powered educational system that turns study materials into interactive learning experiences through multi-agent orchestration, document interaction, and adaptive content generation.
- Designed specialized agents for concept extraction, summarization, and quiz generation
- Built a modular pipeline for comparing web search, LLM synthesis, and hybrid retrieval approaches
- Developed a React-based interface with PDF upload, text selection, and real-time content generation
- Integrated Flask-based backend services and PDF tooling to support downloadable assessments and summaries
- Extended the system with podcast-style audio generation to support multimodal learning

title: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration Platform with Web Integration
description:
Built a public demo showcasing web-native multi-agent AI orchestration, translating ideas from a broader private research effort into a shareable full-stack implementation.
- Integrated AutoGen-based multi-agent workflows into a React frontend with real-time backend communication
- Built a WebSocket layer to support live user interaction without terminal-based dependencies
- Implemented specialized agents for analytical, creative, and media-oriented responses
- Created a custom user-proxy workflow to preserve human-in-the-loop interactions through the web interface
- Added Flask-based media upload support and backend orchestration for multimodal interaction patterns

title: BiLSTM-CRF for Named Entity Recognition
description:
Built a biomedical NER model using a BiLSTM-CRF architecture with character-level CNN features and static word embeddings, focused on sequence labeling for scientific text.
- Modeled entity extraction for DNA, RNA, proteins, cell types, and cell lines on NLPBA 2004 dataset
- Compared CRF, softmax-margin, and SVM-margin losses
- Used padded minibatches, masking, and hyperparameter tuning
- Reached F1 of approximately 0.59

title: Robustness and Domain Adaptation in Transformer-Based QA
description:
Studied the robustness of transformer-based question answering across in-domain, adversarial, and out-of-domain settings, with a focus on adapting RoBERTa to biomedical QA tasks using parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
- Evaluated RoBERTa on SQuAD 2.0 and Covid-QA to analyze robustness under domain shift
- Applied LoRA-based fine-tuning to improve performance on biomedical text while reducing trainable parameters
- Implemented sliding-window inference and CLS-token masking for long contexts and unanswerable questions
- Achieved +6.41 EM / +14.84 F1 on Covid-QA dev set and +5.33 EM / +10.54 F1 on test
- Analyzed failure modes under ambiguity and contradiction

title: UCSC RAG Chatbot
description:
Built a retrieval-augmented chatbot to answer questions about the UCSC NLP Master's Program and related university services. Designed the system to improve answer quality and factual reliability through retrieval, evaluation, and mobile delivery.
- Built a RAG pipeline using LangChain, ChromaDB, and LLaMA 3.2 over 77 scraped UCSC-affiliated web pages
- Integrated Gemini 2.0 Flash as a post-response evaluator to check relevance, coherence, and bias before surfacing answers
- Developed a Swift-based iOS app with Firebase for authentication, chat history, and real-time access
- Evaluated the system on a custom 150-question benchmark, achieving 97% human-evaluated accuracy and 71% strict accuracy under Gemini-based evaluation
- Compared performance across three LLaMA model sizes (1B, 3B, and 8B)

title: Sentiment Analysis Minibatching
description:
Explored the impact of batch size and learning rate on sentiment classification performance using Logistic Regression and LSTM models trained on the IMDB movie review dataset.
- Built sentiment classification pipelines using both Logistic Regression and LSTM on 50,000+ reviews
- Implemented dynamic mini-batch training in PyTorch with custom tokenization
- Benchmarked multiple batch size and learning rate combinations
- Achieved up to 90.4% validation accuracy with LR and 90.1% with LSTM
- Analyzed efficiency-performance tradeoffs including gradient noise

title: Neural Machine Translation: Subword vs. Word-Level Tokenization
description:
Built French-to-English neural machine translation models to compare subword and whole-word tokenization strategies across translation quality, convergence behavior, and vocabulary handling.
- Built Transformer and CNN-based translation models on IWSLT 2013 French-English dataset
- Compared BPE and Moses whole-word tokenization
- Tuned dropout and model depth for BLEU performance
- Evaluated with SacreBLEU and qualitative analysis
- Whole-word tokenization achieved top BLEU of 28.98

title: Modernized ELIZA Rogerian Therapist Chatbot
description:
Modernized the classic ELIZA chatbot with targeted NLP improvements to better handle misspellings, contractions, humor, and flirtatious input while preserving its rule-based structure and therapeutic tone.
- Enhanced a classic rule-based chatbot with modular NLP components
- Built a spell-checking pipeline using TextBlob and spaCy
- Added custom handling for humor, laughter, and flirtatious input
- Implemented contraction-aware lemmatization
- Improved chatbot realism and interaction quality

title: POS Tagging with HMMs and Viterbi Algorithm
description:
Built a probabilistic part-of-speech tagger using a Hidden Markov Model and Viterbi decoding, with a focus on numerical stability, sparse data handling, and efficient sequence inference.
- Implemented an HMM-based POS tagging pipeline with Viterbi decoding
- Used log-space probability computation and add-ε smoothing
- Added fallback handling for unseen word-tag pairs
- Applied dynamic programming and backpointer tracking

title: Autoregressive Language Modeling with Transformers (Penn Treebank)
description:
Built a transformer-based autoregressive language model on the Penn Treebank dataset, focusing on sequence prediction, long-range dependency modeling, and perplexity reduction.
- Implemented a Transformer Encoder with sinusoidal positional encoding and multi-head attention
- Tuned embedding size, batch size, and learning rate
- Reduced test perplexity from 83.35 to 39.11
- Analyzed architecture-performance tradeoffs

title: Slot Tagging with BiLSTM and Attention
description:
Built and tuned slot tagging models using BiLSTM architectures with attention and GloVe embeddings.
- Built slot tagging models using BiLSTM with attention
- Integrated GloVe embeddings and custom layers
- Tuned learning rate, hidden dims, dropout
- Evaluated with token and sequence-level metrics in IOB2
- Achieved 75.37% accuracy, F1 0.950 (sklearn) and 0.839 (seqeval)

title: Building and Evaluating N-Gram Language Models
description:
Built and evaluated unigram, bigram, trigram, and interpolated n-gram language models on a subset of the One Billion Word Benchmark.
- Implemented unigram, bigram, trigram, and interpolated models
- Used linear interpolation with tunable weights
- Added configurable UNK thresholds
- Evaluated across splits showing lower perplexity in higher-order n-grams
- Analyzed data sparsity effects

title: Airline Sentiment Analysis and Preprocessing Pipeline
description:
Built a sentiment classification pipeline for social media text, focused on custom tokenization, preprocessing, and traditional ML-based sentiment modeling on the Twitter US Airline dataset.
- Designed a regex-based tokenizer for social media text including mentions, hashtags, emojis
- Applied 12+ preprocessing steps
- Trained TF-IDF + SVM classifier with ablation studies
- Achieved 80.89% test accuracy
- Conducted user-level sentiment analysis across six airlines

title: Multiclass Text Classification with Feature Engineering
description:
Built and evaluated multiclass text classification models on an e-commerce dataset, focusing on feature engineering, model comparison, and performance tradeoffs.
- Trained and compared SVM, LR, and Decision Tree classifiers
- Engineered features using TF-IDF, GloVe, NER counts, POS ratios, sentiment scores
- Combined BoW and NLTK features
- Conducted hyperparameter tuning
- Found sublinear TF-IDF delivered strongest results

title: Relation Extraction from Natural Language using MLPs
description:
Built a multi-label relation extraction pipeline to identify semantic relations from natural language utterances.
- Built a PyTorch-based MLP for multi-label relation extraction
- Used spaCy embeddings with Freebase-style schema
- Designed two-layer MLP with batch norm and dropout
- Applied stratified K-fold cross-validation
- Achieved ~0.91 F1 and 0.86 accuracy

title: Sentiment Analysis with Custom and Standard ML Models
description:
Built and compared sentiment classification pipelines on Amazon and IMDb reviews using both custom and standard machine learning models.
- Built classifiers using Naive Bayes, SVM, LR, Decision Tree
- Engineered features with BoW, TF-IDF, one-hot, n-grams
- Custom Naive Bayes with Laplacian smoothing reached 85-87%
- SVM+TF-IDF achieved 89% on Amazon
- Evaluated with accuracy, F1, confusion matrices

title: Personal Portfolio
description:
Designed and maintain a responsive personal portfolio to showcase projects, experience, and technical work. Built with React, Node.js, Material UI, and Firebase, with a focus on clean presentation, usability, and cross-device compatibility.
- Built and deployed a responsive web portfolio to highlight projects, experience, and technical skills
- Designed for smooth navigation and consistent UX across desktop and mobile devices
- Added interactive elements including a dynamic photo gallery and direct links to live projects and repositories
- Used the site as an evolving hub for presenting both technical work and creative interests

title: Full-Stack To-Do List Application
description:
Built a full-stack CRUD application using Vue.js and Flask, with a focus on frontend-backend integration, responsive design, and core task management workflows.
- Built a Vue.js frontend and Flask backend for task CRUD
- Implemented server-side data handling and real-time updates
- Used Bootstrap for responsive UI
- Configured CORS for frontend-backend communication

title: Cross-Platform Messaging App
description:
Built a real-time messaging app inspired by Discord and Slack across three platforms: native Android, native iOS, and React Native.
- Built parallel implementations in Kotlin, Swift, and React Native
- Implemented consistent messaging features across platforms
- Used Barista, XCTest, and Jest for testing
- Applied modular design and TDD practices

title: ParkMe – Peer-to-Peer Parking App
description:
Served as Product Owner and full-stack developer for a mobile app that lets users list and rent private parking spaces.
- Led feature scoping, technical planning, and team coordination
- Built backend endpoints for listing management and rental transactions
- Configured Google Cloud and mapping services
- Contributed across the stack using Flutter, Node.js, TypeScript, TSOA, PostgreSQL
- Helped design client-server integration and database structure

title: Multithreaded HTTP Web Server in C
description:
Built a multithreaded HTTP server in C with support for core request handling, concurrent worker threads, and audit logging.
- Implemented GET, PUT, HEAD with status code handling
- Used socket programming and byte-level request parsing
- Built configurable worker thread pools and thread-safe handling
- Added audit logging for debugging and monitoring
- Strengthened systems programming skills

title: Full-Stack Email System
description:
Built a full-stack email platform with support for sending, receiving, and organizing messages.
- Built React frontend and Node.js/Express backend for email management
- Implemented mailbox navigation (inbox, sent, custom folders)
- Designed responsive UI with Material UI
- Built backend APIs and PostgreSQL-based storage
